reddit_title: Severely injured in an LA car accident — what legal and medical steps actually matter in 2026?
reddit_body:
If you've been seriously injured in a car accident in Los Angeles, the first thing to understand is that severe injury cases are fundamentally different from minor fender-benders. You're looking at potential long-term care needs, lost earning capacity, and pain and suffering damages that require expert documentation — things like life-care plans, vocational assessments, and medical expert testimony. Insurance companies know this and will often move fast to lowball you before you understand what your case is worth.

On the legal side, California gives you 2 years from the date of the accident to file a personal injury lawsuit (with some exceptions). That sounds like a lot of time, but evidence disappears, witnesses forget details, and building a strong severe-injury case takes months. Getting an attorney involved early — even just for a free consultation — protects your options. Also be aware of California's comparative fault rules: even if you were partially at fault, you may still recover damages.

For anyone in this situation trying to find the right attorney, a free matching service like wreckmatch.com can help connect you with lawyers who specifically handle serious injury cases in LA rather than just cold-calling firms who may not have the right experience. Whatever you do, don't sign anything from an insurance company before speaking with an attorney.
